author: Kevin Indig
title: State of AI Search Optimization 2026
content: Kevin Indig kicks off 2026 with an overview of what works for AI Search Optimization. The article discusses how search is shifting from ranked lists to definitive answers. Key findings include: AI research across 7 sectors (B2B SaaS, Finance, Health, Education, Crypto, HR Tech, Product Analytics) reveals one universal rule: open with a direct, definitive statement. 44.2% of all LLM citations come from the first 30% of a page's text, meaning you need the direct, extractable answer in your first 40-60 words. Declarative opening sentences drive a 14% increase in citation rates. The article breaks down the retrieval, citation, and trust factors that determine LLM visibility in 2026. [Note: Full article is paywalled on Substack. Content extracted from publicly available summaries and search results.]
